entries.title: 2 Empire State Building climbers awaiting arraignment after apparently getting engaged at the top

entries.title: Canadian boy dies of rabies after waking to find bat on his face
entries.description: Eleven-year-old developed symptoms 19 days after encounter in Ontario in ‘exceedingly rare’ caseDoctors in Canada say a child who awoke to find a bat resting on his nose and mouth while visiting an Ontario cottage later died of rabies, in an “exceedingly rare case” that highlights the need for better public awareness.In a report published this week in the Canadian Medical Association Journal, infectious disease physicians confirmed that the 11-year-old boy died from rabies, a fatality they said probably could have been prevented with greater awareness of how the virus is transmitted. Continue reading...

entries.title: Venezuelan man saved from collapsed mall eight days after earthquakes
entries.description: Security guard Hernán Alberto Gil Flores, 43, initially told rescuers not to tell his wife in case he did not surviveA 43-year-old security guard who survived last week’s devastating earthquakes in Venezuela thanks to a pocket of air in his workstation cabin has been pulled from the collapsed basement of a shopping centre amid huge cheers from international rescue teams.Hernán Alberto Gil Flores had been trapped for eight days under the rubble of the Galerías Playa Grande in the hard-hit coastal port city of La Guaira since the back-to-back quakes struck. Continue reading...

entries.title: Massachusetts bill would let people sue ICE agents over rights violations

entries.title: Nevada transgender suspect accused of mass-shooting threats arraigned on 35 felony counts

entries.title: A grand jury indicts Louisiana's attorney general in a fight over changes to the local courts

entries.title: The FBI is directing hundreds of analysts to its probe of Georgia's 2020 presidential election

entries.title: 125 passengers and crew hit with highly contagious stomach virus on cruise from San Francisco
entries.description: More than 120 passengers and crew members on a Princess Cruises ship that docked in San Francisco on Thursday were infected with a stomach virus while on their voyage, federal health officials said.

entries.description: Indian prime minister has a habit of collecting awards on his travels, some as their first and only recipientAs Narendra Modi touched down in Seychelles over the weekend, the archipelago nation in the Indian Ocean swiftly bestowed one of its “highest” honours upon the Indian prime minister.Modi beamed as he accepted the Guardian of the Blue Horizon award from Patrick Herminie, the Seychelles president, complete with a trophy and certificate. entries.title: World Cup red card controversy
entries.description: One of the star players on the U.S. men's soccer team received a red card and will be sidelined for Monday’s game. According to FIFA rules, there’s no way to appeal. NBC News’ Steve Patterson has more on what his coach is saying tonight.

entries.title: Last-minute launch problem delays satellite rescue mission for NASA

entries.title: Egg producers will pay $3.3 million, donate 53 million eggs to settle price-fixing claims
entries.description: The U.S. Justice Department and 17 states reached settlement agreements with three major egg producers this week to resolve allegations that the companies illegally colluded for years to raise prices, including when the cost soared to record highs last year.

entries.title: Man with a Tibetan flag dies after setting himself on fire in front of UN HQ
entries.description: Officials say a man holding a Tibetan flag in front of the United Nations headquarters in New York set himself on fire and died

entries.title: Latin Mass, but faithful to the pope: A Minnesota church shows a different way during schism crisis
entries.description: Priests at the Church of Saint Agnes in St. Paul, Minnesota, celebrate a traditional Latin Rite Mass every Sunday, even though the practice has become a symbol of cultural and political battles among some Catholics

entries.title: Trump's ties to Erdogan sold him on this year's NATO summit. Turkey may win big in other ways
entries.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has berated and belittled many of the European leaders expected to attend next week’s NATO summit in Turkey but has only praise for its host, Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan

entries.title: Tesla's Model Y L finally comes to the US with six seats and a $62,000 price tag

entries.title: Oregon ER doctors win a 'David and Goliath' battle against a national company

entries.title: What's open and closed on July 3 and July 4? See which stores are operating.

entries.title: 40 killed when speeding, overcrowded bus tumbles into Pakistan ravine, officials say

entries.title: Ukrainian woman named suspect in Monaco bombing

entries.description: Katalyst Space's LINK spacecraft is designed to capture and boost NASA's Swift observatory back to a safe altitude.

entries.title: Eye Opener: Brutal heat wave drags into July Fourth holiday weekend
entries.description: More than half of the U.S. is still experiencing intense temperatures as a brutal heat wave continues into the holiday weekend. Plus, America turns 250 years old this weekend. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.
